summary: Gruber dissects an NPD Group report on U.S. tablet sales that excludes iPad numbers to make the non-Apple tablet market appear competitive. He reconstructs the full picture by estimating U.S. iPad sales at 10 million units, revealing Apple held roughly 89% market share while second-place HP sold only about 204,000 units during a fire sale. He argues NPD's framing of 'non-Apple tablets' as a meaningful category is deliberately misleading, and that the real lesson is PC manufacturers are failing in tablets while ecosystem-driven companies succeed.
key_points: NPD's report excluded iPad sales entirely, creating an artificial 'non-Apple tablet' category that obscured Apple's ~89% U.S. market share
Apple sold roughly 50 times more tablets than second-place HP, whose sales were driven by a fire sale on a discontinued product
NPD's claim that 76% of non-Apple tablet buyers didn't consider the iPad actually means 97% of people who considered an iPad bought one
The report also excluded Barnes & Noble's Nook, which likely outsold all the listed PC manufacturers combined
PC manufacturers are failing in tablets; the market belongs to companies offering complete ecosystems of hardware, software, and content
money_quotes: What makes this whole presentation so contorted is the notion of 'non-Apple tablets' as a separate product category, without any context as to where these tablets stand when the iPad is included.
NPD is trying to paint a picture that there's a contest going on where there is none.
97 percent of tablet buyers who merely considered an iPad bought an iPad.
If not for the 8 percent of tablet buyers who for whatever reason did not consider an iPad, none of these companies would have sold even 100,000 tablets over the first nine months of 2011.
PC manufacturers are not dominant in the tablet space. Companies that provide a complete ecosystem — hardware, software, app stores, movies, TV shows, books and periodicals — are.
PC manufacturers are utterly failing in the tablet market.
The only thing you can learn from NPD's report is that tablet market share numbers sure do look different when you don't count any of the tablets that people are actually buying.
key_insight: Excluding Apple from tablet market data to manufacture a competitive narrative only reveals how completely the iPad dominates — and how badly PC manufacturers are failing to compete.
